title: Summit Therapeutics — Ivonescimab Nov 2026 PDUFA + $100M Co-CEO Cluster Buy
catalyst: Co-CEOs Robert Duggan and Maky Zanganeh each purchased 3.81M shares ($49.99M each, ~$100M combined) on June 12, 2026, at $13.12. Ivonescimab BLA for first-line NSCLC is under FDA review with a November 2026 PDUFA date. HARMONi-6 Phase III showed ivonescimab+chemo cut risk of death by 34% vs tislelizumab combos in squamous NSCLC; data published in The Lancet and highlighted at ASCO 2026.
thesis: Two co-CEOs putting $50M of personal capital into the stock the same day, with a dated regulatory catalyst <6 months out and validated Phase III data, is one of the strongest insider-conviction signals you can construct. Summit owns ex-Greater China rights to ivonescimab from Akeso; approval would establish it as a credible PD-1/VEGF bispecific franchise. The screener flags SMMT as WATCH (IOWN 43), reflecting pre-revenue biotech risk — but the asymmetry comes from validated late-stage data plus a regulatory inflection, not from current fundamentals.

source.title: SMMT Jumps As Ivonescimab Trial Success Fuels Financing Drive

what_would_promote: Confirmed PDUFA date acceptance and AdCom decision (Q3 2026); construction of an entry/target/stop range grounded in a binary-outcome valuation framework (approval upside / CRL downside) before the Sept/Oct AdCom window. A second primary source — the FDA acceptance letter or BLA confirmation from a Summit IR release — would solidify the catalyst date.
checks_remaining: Trade construction not built (binary biotech sizing requires a separate position framework). Only 1 source attached so far; need 1 more primary (Summit IR press release or SEC Form 4 filings) before promoting to On Our Radar.

title: NCLH — Pagliuca/Klarman Insider Stack on Elliott-Led Turnaround
catalyst: Director Stephen Pagliuca (Bain Capital co-chair, appointed via the Elliott Investment Management agreement) purchased 1.38M shares on June 1-2, 2026, for ~$25M at $18-18.16. CEO Harry Sommer purchased 150K+ shares for $2.5M+ in late May. Seth Klarman's Baupost is reported as also buying. 7-insider cluster surfaced in openinsider — total ~$29M across cluster filings.
thesis: Activist Elliott pushed Pagliuca onto the board; FY26 guidance was cut to reset the bar; Pagliuca is now putting his own $25M behind the 'Norwegian Now' multi-year turnaround plan. A respected value investor backing a turnaround with this much personal capital, alongside Baupost-class accumulators, is the kind of setup that gets re-rated 12-24 months out if the operational plan delivers. Cruise demand is recovering and the sector is consolidated. The screener has NCLH, so we can run the dimension scoring.

source.title: Insider Buying: Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) Director Purchases 685,000 Shares

what_would_promote: Confirmation of specific operating milestones in the 'Norwegian Now' plan (margin guidance, cost program targets); a primary source from NCLH's Q2 earnings (late July 2026) that aligns with the turnaround thesis; clearer view of the Elliott/Pagliuca governance changes converting to operational change. Promotion to On Our Radar after Q2 print if guidance is reaffirmed.
checks_remaining: No falsifiable date catalyst yet — turnaround is a multi-quarter story without a single inflection. Need a 2nd primary source (Q2 earnings transcript, Elliott letter, or 13F confirming Baupost accumulation). Trade construction would need to model normalized EBITDA margins under the plan.

title: Amrize (AMRZ) — North American Cement for the Data Center Buildout
catalyst: 4-insider cluster buy ($2.7M) at Amrize surfaced in openinsider on 2026-06-15. AMRZ is the recently spun-out (June 2025) North American cement, aggregates and building envelope business of Holcim, led by former Holcim CEO Jan Jenisch as Chairman and CEO. Hyperscaler 2026 capex was revised UP this quarter to ~$630-650B (Microsoft $190B, Alphabet $180-190B), and each GW of new data center capacity consumes thousands of tonnes of cement for foundations, vaults and pads.
thesis: Amrize is a pureplay on US-led building materials demand at the exact moment the AI data center buildout is driving the largest non-residential construction wave in two decades. Insider buying from a fresh public-company management team with credibility (Jenisch ran Holcim 2017-2024 to outperformance) suggests they see the cement order book firming up. This is a complementary angle to the active `data-center-grid-buildout` and `ai-power-bottleneck` opps — the cement layer underneath the steel-and-copper layer those cover. IOWN screener rates AMRZ HOLD/66, sleeve Growth.

source.title: Swiss cement giant Holcim completes North American spinoff

what_would_promote: An AMRZ Q3 earnings print (or analyst day) that quantifies the data center / non-residential infrastructure mix of orders; a primary source tying specific hyperscaler buildouts to AMRZ-supplied cement/aggregates; a second insider cluster filing within 60 days that reaffirms the conviction.
checks_remaining: Only 1 secondary source attached (Manufacturing Dive). Need 1 primary source (AMRZ Q1 2026 earnings release, IR materials, or SEC Form 4 filings linked to the cluster buy) before promoting to On Our Radar. No trade construction.
